title: Aerangis articulata
intro: Aerangis articulata is a species of orchid native to tropical Africa, known for its unique, white and yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Bioactive constituents of Aerangis articulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aerangis articulata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the production of capsules and tinctures.
taxonomy: Aerangis articulata is a species of orchid, specifically classified within the family Orchidaceae. This classification is further narrowed down to the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Vandeae. At the genus level, Aerangis articulata is a member of the genus Aerangis, which comprises approximately 30 species of orchids. These orchids are commonly known as "star orchids" due to the shape of their flowers. Aerangis articulata's classification is a combination of its botanical and phylogenetic characteristics.
